Just buy your tickets online ahead of time.  You don't need a "tour".  
Your purchase will include the Zoo, the "Children's Zoo (petting zoo) and also a 5-D movie.  This is worth the expense.  
I was shocked how extensive the zoo was, much better than I recalled.  The kids (aged 8 and 13) had a great time for approximately 2.5 hours of fun.  Good for all ages.  There are a couple of feeding events, and the animals are in close proximity so the children could easily see them.  Highly recommend.

My family (2 adults and 2 older teens) love visiting zoos when we travel and this one is iconic.  As others have noted, this zoo is very small. It took us less than an hour to experience the main zoo area that included extended time watching the bears and the red panda.  We wanted to visit the zoo gift shop that is just outside of the zoo and come back to watch the sea lion feeding that was scheduled for 20 minutes later but found out there was no reentry which is surprising since the gift shop and the Tirsh Children’s zoo cannot be accessed from the main zoo.  That being said, the sea lions can easily be seen from the path outside the main zoo.  In the end I’m glad we did it, but definitely manage your expectations.

When my kids were small there was a movie and show called Penguins of Madagascar that was supposed to be set here. The way they portrayed the way it looked was actually pretty accurate. The primary part of the zoo is set up with brick buildings or walls on three sides - with a few indoor buildings and about a dozen outdoor enclosures. The children's zoo - which is really part of the Central Park Zoo is across from the entrance and has petting/feeding with goats, zebus, and a half-dozen other enclosures. All-in-all, took about 90 minutes to see it all.
